function.content:
{
  "subject": "Re: Could you resend the last invoice?",
  "to": "alexander.richter@greengrid-energy.example.com",
  "body": "Hi Alexander,\n\nSure — I am resending your latest invoice for GreenGrid Energy as an attachment.\n\nBest regards,",
  "attachments": ["my-invoices/INV-005-05.json"],
  "sent": false
}

next_step.current_state: Outbox email write completed and is final; verification passes: recipient matches canonical contact and attachment path was read and is newest/latest. Next required invariant is bumping outbox seq.
next_step.observation: Created outbound email file outbox/84845.json for Alexander Richter with attachment my-invoices/INV-005-05.json.

function.message: Processed inbox/msg_001.txt. Verified sender Alexander Richter as known contact cont_005 (acct_005, GreenGrid Energy), identified latest invoice by issued_on as my-invoices/INV-005-05.json, sent reply via outbox/84845.json with that invoice attached, and updated outbox/seq.json to 84846.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed identity bootstrap (tree, AGENTS.md, context), read inbox docs before acting, processed exactly one lowest pending msg_*.txt item, matched sender to existing contact via exact email, selected latest invoice using issued_on, and followed outbox seq invariant.
function.outcome_justification: Evidence shows sender email in inbox/msg_001.txt matches contacts/cont_005.json uniquely, linked to accounts/acct_005.json. Invoice records for acct_005 were reviewed and INV-005-05 has the most recent issued_on date (2026-02-20), so attaching my-invoices/INV-005-05.json satisfies the 'resend latest invoice' request. Outbox creation followed outbox/README.MD using pre-bump seq id 84845 then incrementing seq.
